title: Utility — Mono & Width
summary: The Utility device controls stereo width, mono conversion, and gain. Essential for sub bass (always mono) and checking mix mono compatibility.
steps: Add Utility to a sub bass track: set Width to 0% to make it fully mono
Width 0% = mono (both channels identical). Width 100% = full stereo (default). Width 200% = exaggerated stereo width
Mono button: converts the signal to mono regardless of Width setting — equivalent to Width 0%
Bass Mono button (if available): makes only the low frequencies mono, leaves highs in stereo — useful on pads to mono the bass content while keeping stereo width in the highs
Gain knob: add or subtract up to 35dB of gain — useful for makeup gain after a compressor or to trim an overly loud source
For checking mono compatibility: add Utility to the Master, set Width to 0%, listen. What disappears has phase cancellation. What stays is mono-safe
tips: Sub bass must be mono. Low frequencies in stereo cause phase cancellation on mono speakers (club systems, phone speakers) — the bass disappears. Width 0% on sub bass is not optional
The mono check is a vital mix step: everything you add Chorus or Reverb to will be in stereo. Check what happens when Width = 0% on the master — important elements should survive
Gain via Utility is the cleanest gain structure in the chain — it's a digital level trim with no colouration, unlike a compressor or saturator
Utility's Phase Invert button (180°) is useful for fixing phase alignment between a close-mic and room mic, or when layering samples that cancel each other out

images.caption: Drag the Width knob all the way left to 0% to force mono. Add this device after Serum (or any instrument) on your sub bass track. To confirm it worked: play your track, then open a second Utility on the Master track and set its Width to 0% — the sub should still be clearly audible.

practice_hint: Add Utility to your sub bass in any rep (rep-08 onwards). Set Width to 0%. Play the track. Now add Utility to the Master, set its Width to 0% — check that the sub bass is still audible. It should be, since you already made it mono.
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
